review_scope: Review whether the first readable-doc bundle materially improved PM and stakeholder communication readiness.

target_outcomes.expected_signal: A PM-facing readable bundle should be available on the same working day after source-artifact approval.
target_outcomes.observed_signal: The first discovery, roadmap, and PRD docs now exist and are traceable, but external-publishing metadata is still incomplete.

target_outcomes.expected_signal: Stakeholders should not need the PM to narrate raw JSON into a reviewable document.
target_outcomes.observed_signal: Manual validation says the new docs are internally usable, but the discovery summary and publishing rules still need another iteration.

claim_verification_summary: The readable-doc slice improved internal review readiness, but external publishing and sync claims still need tighter proof.
adoption_notes: The readable-doc bundle is good enough for internal PM and leadership review.
The document-system slice should continue until publishing metadata and sync rules are fully stable.
unresolved_pain_points: External SharePoint and Confluence sync rules are not yet encoded in the generated control artifacts.

support_signals.summary: Support-style feedback still clusters around missing external publishing and sync details.

adoption_signals.signal_name: Readable docs adopted for internal review
adoption_signals.summary: PM and leadership review can now happen from governed readable docs instead of raw JSON alone.

reopen_recommendations.rationale: Publishing and sync details should feed back into the readable-doc PRD and release-boundary scope.

next_action: Keep the readable-doc system active, close the metadata and sync-rule gaps, and refresh the outcome review at the next benchmark window.
